era: 1981-present. Crossover Thrash. E=9 -- hardcore velocity + metal riffing + feral vocals. Oak Harbor, Whidbey Island. Combat Records.

theory_nodes.concept: Crossover thrash genre definition: hardcore tempos (180-220+ BPM) + metal riffing + feral vocal delivery + short songs (1:30-2:30). The Accused among the genre's defining originators alongside D.R.I., C.O.C., Suicidal Tendencies.
theory_nodes.description: The crossover thrash template fully documented in The Accused's Combat records.

theory_nodes.concept: Oak Harbor / Naval Air Station Whidbey Island context. EA-18G Growler aircraft as acoustic background. Island isolation + military-adjacent working-class community = extreme music origin. FIRST OAK HARBOR ENTRY.
theory_nodes.description: The most geographically remote source location in the engine. Island community under the Growlers' flight path.

theory_nodes.concept: Martha Splatterhead persona: female horror-comedy mascot, 40+ year brand persistence. Thrash metal mascot tradition (Iron Maiden Eddie) applied with punk irreverence and splatter film aesthetics.
theory_nodes.description: Martha Splatterhead enters the engine at degree 329.

theory_nodes.concept: Combat Records ecosystem: Megadeth, Exodus, Possessed, Dark Angel, S.O.D., The Accused. The commercial infrastructure for American extreme music when major labels refused it. Northwest voice in the national extreme catalog.
theory_nodes.description: Combat Records as the extreme metal network's distribution and marketing platform.

cross_references.relationship: Metal arc continues. E=9 crossover velocity to E=8 progressive metal precision. Oak Harbor island to Bellevue Eastside suburb. Combat Records to EMI.

E=9 pair. FIRST INSECT SOUND in SPS engine. Two simultaneous E=9 events: crossover thrash velocity and mass insect emergence wing-buzz along an entire river corridor. The engine's most explosive simultaneous pairing.
